title: RPM Package
documentation:
## Purpose
RPM (Red Hat Package Manager) is the binary package format for Red Hat-based Linux distributions (RHEL, Fedora, CentOS, Rocky Linux, openSUSE, etc.). An `.rpm` file contains a lead section, signature, header metadata, and a compressed CPIO payload. The [RPM Package](https://capdag.com/browse/media:rpm;archive) media type represents any `.rpm` file.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes RPM-family Linux packages — for example, software build and release pipelines, private YUM/DNF repository management, deployment automation, or security and compliance scanning workflows.

## Notes
RPM packages use a custom binary format; do not attempt to parse them as ZIP or tar archives. Implementers should use `rpm2cpio` to extract the CPIO payload or use the `rpm` command-line tools and `rpm-python` bindings for metadata access. Packages are signed with GPG; verification requires importing the publisher's public key via `rpm --import`. The `;archive` tag indicates this is a packaged archive artifact.
